skill: Command of Evidence — which detail supports the claim
source: Derived from error patterns across CB SAT R&W practice (data/cb-corpus/)

mistakeCategories.name: True statement that doesn't support THIS claim
mistakeCategories.description: Option is factually accurate per the passage but addresses a different point than the claim in the question.
mistakeCategories.studentExample: Claim is about cost; distractor cites a true detail about timing.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Write a distractor that is true per the passage but supports a DIFFERENT claim than the one asked about.

mistakeCategories.name: Evidence that undermines rather than supports
mistakeCategories.description: Student picks a detail that actually weakens or contradicts the claim.
mistakeCategories.studentExample: Claim: the method is efficient. Distractor: a detail showing it is slow.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Write a distractor that is on-topic but points the OPPOSITE direction from the claim.

mistakeCategories.name: Over-broad statement not grounded in the passage
mistakeCategories.description: Option makes a sweeping generalization the passage never establishes.
mistakeCategories.studentExample: Passage describes one study; distractor claims 'all researchers agree'.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Write a distractor that overgeneralizes beyond what the passage actually states.

mistakeCategories.name: Plausible real-world fact not in the passage
mistakeCategories.description: Option is reasonable general knowledge but unsupported by the text.
mistakeCategories.studentExample: A commonly-known fact about the topic that the passage never mentions.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Write a distractor that is plausible outside knowledge but is NOT stated or implied in the passage.
